candidates.why: Primary goods revenue line; largest recurring revenue component and best proxy for top-line sales among candidates. Balancing value is an adjustment and not the main revenue.
Additional revenue stream but smaller and more ancillary than main goods revenue; excluded to avoid double-counting REVT as specification typically expects a single top-line row.
Clearly labeled as a balancing value with negligible amounts; not an economic revenue line.

variables.notes: Chose the main product revenue line as REVT; other business-related activities are ancillary and excluded to keep a single top-line figure.

candidates.why: Direct cost of inputs to production; clearly fits cost of goods sold / cost of sales definition better than inventory balance sheet items.
Inventory asset, not an expense; therefore not appropriate for COGS.

candidates.row_label: Operating materials and spare parts - Net
candidates.why: Also an inventory/asset account; not a P&L expense line.
This is income (often negative expense) rather than SG&A expense; excluded per instructions to focus on overhead expense components.
Duplicate concept of other operating income; also income rather than SG&A expense; excluded.
Stock-based compensation, which is a classic SG&A component when not included in COGS; should be included as an SG&A component.
Explicit overhead/operating expense bucket; fits SG&A (selling, general and administrative/other operating expenses).
Line label for other operating expense; same nature as previous but separately broken out and thus also part of SG&A components.

variables.notes: Excluded income-type rows and selected only expense-side overhead components, including stock-based compensation and other operating expenses.

candidates.row_label: Research & Development Expense - Government Grants - Supplemental
candidates.why: Represents grants offsetting R&D rather than the gross expense; not the best stand-alone R&D expense measure.
Represents capitalized portion of R&D; not the period expense itself.
Explicit R&D expense line (supplemental disclosure) best representing the R&D expense for the period.
Balance sheet capitalized development costs; not a period expense.

candidates.row_label: New products development- Accumulated Amortization & Impairment
candidates.why: Accumulated amortization/impairment on capitalized development; not the current period R&D expense line.

candidates.row_label: Change in fair value commodity contracts interest element
candidates.why: Derivative fair value change, not traditional interest expense on debt.

candidates.row_label: Write-down / reversal of write down of interest in other companies
candidates.why: Impairment/reversal on investments, not interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense - Net of Capitalized Interest
candidates.why: Direct interest expense line net of capitalized interest; primary candidate for XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Capital Financing - Gross
candidates.why: Gross interest on capital financing; also a clear interest expense component.
Interest capitalized to assets, typically excluded from P&L interest expense for XINT; not selected.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest on lease liabilities; economically an interest expense and part of XINT.
Represents profit attribution, not interest expense.
Same as above; equity attribution, not interest.

candidates.row_label: Non-controlling interests share of comprehensive income
candidates.why: Comprehensive income attribution to minorities; not interest.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of the same lease interest; main P&L line already captured.

candidates.row_label: Interest in associates and other companies
candidates.why: Investment in associates; balance sheet asset, not interest expense.
Debt balance, not expense.
Debt balance, not expense.
Short-term debt, not expense.
Accrued liability for interest but not the expense line itself; less direct than P&L interest expense rows.
Portion of short-term debt; not the expense.
Equity component; not interest.
Equity minority interest; not interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense - Net of Capitalized Interest
Interest Expense on Capital Financing - Gross
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities

variables.notes: Selected all clear P&L interest expense components and excluded capitalized interest and non-interest items.

candidates.row_label: Share of profit(loss) from equity accounted investments
candidates.why: Income statement share of profits; not an equity stock measure.

candidates.row_label: Share of other comprehensive income (loss) from equity accounted companies
candidates.why: OCI share; not total book equity.

candidates.row_label: Share of profit(loss) from equity accounted financial investment
candidates.why: Again a P&L item, not equity.

candidates.row_label: Change in fair value of equity instruments
candidates.why: Fair value movement, not aggregate equity total.

candidates.row_label: Share of other comprehensive income (loss) from equity accounted investments
candidates.why: OCI share; not equity level.
Investment asset; not total equity of the company.
Represents equity attributable to owners of the parent (excl. non-controlling interests); aligns with book equity/shareholders’ equity definition.
Includes non-controlling interests (slightly higher than Total Equity); more like total equity including minorities, whereas BE usually targets parent equity when MIB is modeled separately.

variables.notes: Chose parent shareholders’ equity (Total Equity) rather than Total equity including non-controlling interests, since MIB is modeled separately.

candidates.why: Flow measure of minority interest in profit; useful but MIB for balance sheet typically focuses on stock, not flow.
Appears to duplicate the non-controlling interests share of profit; also a flow item.

candidates.row_label: Non-controlling interests share of comprehensive income
candidates.why: Flow of comprehensive income to minorities; not the balance sheet value.
Balance sheet line for minority interest / non-controlling interests; best match for MIB definition.
Duplicate label for the same balance sheet item; but “Non-controlling interests” is already clear and standard IFRS wording.
